bins.name: Supports the rulemaking
bins.desc: ACORE backs FERC opening a new rulemaking on large load interconnection and calls the ANOPR an important step.
quotes: ACORE supports the initiation of a new Commission rulemaking on the interconnection of large loads

bins.name: Integrate planning, expand the grid
bins.desc: ACORE urges FERC to go beyond interconnection and integrate large load and generation interconnection needs into long-term transmission planning, working with DOE and the national labs on common load-forecasting methods.
quotes: we urge the Commission to avoid focusing solely on interconnection and instead take steps to expand and modernize the transmission system to achieve the greatest cost savings and reliability benefits.

the Commission to work with DOE and the National Laboratories to establish best practices and common methodologies for the forecasting of large loads and their power needs that can be incorporated into the long-term transmission planning process.
An essential component of improving the interconnection of large loads will be to fully integrate interconnection-driven needs into longer-term transmission planning.

bins.name: Transmission expansion lowers cost
bins.desc: ACORE argues that expanding high-voltage transmission and deploying grid enhancing technologies and high-performance conductors achieves cost savings and reliability benefits while meeting large load demand.
quotes: we urge the Commission to avoid focusing solely on interconnection and instead take steps to expand and modernize the transmission system to achieve the greatest cost savings and reliability benefits.
An expansion of transmission, especially long-range and high-voltage lines, is well demonstrated to achieve cost savings.
Essential to cost-effectively meeting the large load needs is an accelerated deployment of Grid Enhancing Technologies and High-Performance Conductors.

bins.name: Caution on expedited processing
bins.desc: ACORE wants a longer rulemaking timeline rather than the April 30, 2026 deadline, opposes a serial study process, and warns that expediting curtailable load interconnections could divert resources from generation already waiting in the queue.
quotes: ACORE recommends that if the Commission were to establish such a rulemaking, it does so with a reasonable timeline that allows for a sufficient evidentiary record and the development of informed and comprehensive
ACORE is concerned that queue prioritization methods can disrupt the processing of needed generation waiting in lengthy interconnection queues.
The Commission should avoid a serial interconnection process for large load interconnection.

bins.name: Allow a fuller record
bins.desc: ACORE asks FERC to set a reasonable timeline allowing a sufficient evidentiary record, instead of rushing to a final rule by the ANOPR's April 30, 2026 target.
quotes: ACORE recommends that if the Commission were to establish such a rulemaking, it does so with a reasonable timeline that allows for a sufficient evidentiary record and the development of informed and comprehensive

bins.name: Study load and generation together
bins.desc: ACORE agrees with studying load and hybrid facilities alongside generating facilities to improve efficiency and lower network upgrade costs, and wants common forecasting methods built into planning.
quotes: the Commission to work with DOE and the National Laboratories to establish best practices and common methodologies for the forecasting of large loads and their power needs that can be incorporated into the long-term transmission planning process.
ACORE agrees with the ANOPR recommendations to study load and hybrid facilities together with generating facilities as a means to improve the process efficiency and lower network upgrade costs

bins.name: Cut upgrade cost via advanced tech
bins.desc: ACORE ties lower network upgrade costs to joint study and accelerated deployment of grid enhancing technologies, high-performance conductors, and an enumerated list of alternative transmission technologies.
quotes: Essential to cost-effectively meeting the large load needs is an accelerated deployment of Grid Enhancing Technologies and High-Performance Conductors.
ACORE agrees with the ANOPR recommendations to study load and hybrid facilities together with generating facilities as a means to improve the process efficiency and lower network upgrade costs

One gap in the ANOPR principles that should be incorporated into a rulemaking on the interconnection of large loads is to mirror Order No. 2023’s requirements for “transmission providers to evaluate the following enumerated list of alternative

bins.name: Include hybrid facilities
bins.desc: ACORE supports studying hybrid facilities together with load and generation as part of an efficient interconnection process.
quotes: ACORE agrees with the ANOPR recommendations to study load and hybrid facilities together with generating facilities as a means to improve the process efficiency and lower network upgrade costs

bins.name: Standardized deposits and readiness
bins.desc: ACORE supports standardized study deposits, readiness requirements, and withdrawal penalties for load and hybrid facilities to give greater certainty that interconnection requests will materialize.
quotes: to subject load and hybrid facilities to standardized study deposits, readiness requirements, and withdrawal penalties to provide greater certainty on the likelihood that such interconnection requests will materialize.

bins.name: Curtailable load tradeoffs
bins.desc: ACORE is wary that prioritizing curtailable or flexible loads through queue prioritization could disrupt processing of generation needed to meet growing demand.
quotes: ACORE is concerned that queue prioritization methods can disrupt the processing of needed generation waiting in lengthy interconnection queues.

bins.name: Avoid serial study
bins.desc: ACORE opposes a serial interconnection process for large loads, citing Order No. 2023's finding that serial study creates inefficiencies and queue backlogs.
quotes: The Commission should avoid a serial interconnection process for large load interconnection.

bins.name: Enumerate advanced transmission tech
bins.desc: ACORE wants the rulemaking to mirror Order No. 2023's enumerated list of alternative transmission technologies and to add dynamic line ratings, so transmission providers evaluate these options.
quotes: One gap in the ANOPR principles that should be incorporated into a rulemaking on the interconnection of large loads is to mirror Order No. 2023’s requirements for “transmission providers to evaluate the following enumerated list of alternative
We also urge the Commission to include dynamic line ratings in the list of enumerated technologies.
